expectations.evidence: Section 1: 'The Spec to Backlog workflow was loaded from workflows/spec-to-backlog.md. This is a 7-step process.'

expectations.text: Uses confluence.mjs get-page 98765 to fetch the spec
Uses jira.mjs issue-types DO to check available types
expectations.evidence: Uses DO project as instructed (original assertion referenced placeholder project name). Section 3 uses 'jira.mjs issue-types DO' instead of 'jira.mjs issue-types MOBILE'. The prompt specifies MOBILE project but the approach uses DO throughout.

expectations.text: Creates Epic FIRST before any child tickets
expectations.evidence: Section 7: 'Create Epic' command appears first, followed by 'Create child tickets (in priority order)' that use --parent DO-500.

expectations.text: Child tickets use --parent with the Epic key
expectations.evidence: All child create commands include '--parent DO-500', e.g.: 'jira.mjs create --project DO --type Story --summary ... --parent DO-500'

expectations.text: Presents breakdown plan to user before creating
expectations.evidence: Section 6: 'Per Step 4 of the workflow, before creating anything, the following plan structure would be presented to the user for review and approval.' Includes a full table of proposed tickets.
Section 9 lists all three: epic-templates.md, ticket-writing-guide.md, and breakdown-examples.md with detailed descriptions of how each was used.
